titleStmt.title: A letter from John Furlong to Matthew Nathan, 17 December 1915
Letters 1916-1923
titleStmt.author: John Pedder Furlong

editionStmt.p: This work was originally published by Maynooth University in Ireland in 2017. In 2026 this data, stored in a relational database was extracted and converted into this TEI/XML document.

availability.p: This is an open access work licensed under Creative Commons Attribution NonCommercial 4.0 International license (CC BY-NC 4.0).

note.p: A letter from John Pedder Furlong to Under-Secretary for Ireland Sir Matthew Nathan acknowledging receiving a salary.John Pedder Furlong was an estate owner and High Sheriff of County Waterford. Sir Matthew Nathan (1862-1939) served as the under secretary for Ireland from 1914 to 1916. In 1916 Nathan offered his resignation following the outbreak of the Easter Rising.

msIdentifier.collection: Chief Secretary Office Registered Papers,, CSO_RP_!916_164_20_19

projectDesc.p: The data in these XML files was generated based on a data dump from the Magellan database (https://github.com/Maynooth-Center-for-Digital-Humanities/Magellan). Each database record has been converted to a JSON file (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/json). The element section of the JSON file contains the TEI-encoded contents of the letters. The TEI XML has been cleaned and made well-formed using a Python script (https://github.com/letters1916static/letters-data/tree/main/src).

body.ab:
BALLINAPARKA, CAPPOQUIN. 17th Dec 1915 Dear Sir, In reply to yours
of yesterdays date on other
other side you will see
the voucher. Yours truly J. Peddar Furlong The Under Secretary Dublin Castle. Ballinaparka,
Cappoquin
17th Dec 1915 Received from Under Secretary
Dublin Castle the sum of eighty seven
pounds. 10/- being the second moiety
for salary for Under Sheriff for
the Co Waterford. J. Peddar Furlong High Sheriff
Co Waterford
